title: TC001-Hero renders with name gradient text and primary CTAs
description: Verifies the home hero section loads and shows the key identity text and CTA buttons.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Verify hero section is visible by checking a specific element within the hero (svg)
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[1]/div[2]/div/div[4]/svg').is_visible(), 'Hero section (svg) is not visible'
        # The test plan requests verifying the text "Shafiqul Islam Sakib" is visible.
        # No element in the provided available elements list contains the exact text "Shafiqul Islam Sakib".
        # According to the test plan instructions, if a feature does not exist, report the issue and stop.
        raise AssertionError('Element containing text "Shafiqul Islam Sakib" was not found in the available elements; aborting the task.')
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Navigate to Skills via 'View My Work' CTA
description: Verifies the 'View My Work' button routes the user from the hero section to the Skills page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'View My Work' button in the hero section (element index 103).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Return to the homepage by clicking the 'Home' link so the hero section (with the 'View My Work' button) is visible, then click the 'View My Work' button to verify it routes to /skills.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Home' link to return to the homepage, then click the 'View My Work' button in the hero to verify it routes to /skills.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'View My Work' button in the hero section (element index 714) to trigger navigation to the Skills page, then verify the URL and page content.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Home' link to return to the homepage so the hero section (with the 'View My Work' button) is visible, then locate and click the hero 'View My Work' button to trigger navigation to /skills and verify the three assertions.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Home' link to return to the homepage so the hero section (with the 'View My Work' button) is visible. After navigation, locate and click the hero 'View My Work' button and then verify: (1) URL contains '/skills', (2) page title contains 'Skills', (3) Skills page heading is visible. Then finish with done.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'View My Work' button in the hero section (element index 1201) to trigger navigation to the Skills page, then verify URL contains '/skills', page title contains 'Skills', and that the Skills page heading is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Home' link (element index 60) to return to the homepage so the hero section with the 'View My Work' button is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Home link (index 57) to return to the homepage so the hero section with the 'View My Work' button is visible. After returning home, locate and click the hero 'View My Work' button and then verify the three assertions (URL contains '/skills', page title contains 'Skills', Skills page heading visible). Immediate action: click element index 57.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the hero 'View My Work' button (element index 1732) to attempt navigation to /skills, then verify: (1) URL contains '/skills', (2) page title contains 'Skills', (3) Skills page heading is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div/div[2]/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/skills' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Skills').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//h1[contains(., "Skills")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Hero 'View My Work' button did not navigate to /skills after clicking; URL remains http://localhost:3000/.
- Page title does not contain 'Skills' and the Skills page heading is not visible after interacting with the hero button.
- Multiple click attempts on different hero button element indexes (103, 714, 1201, 1732) did not trigger navigation to /skills.

title: TC004-Navigate to Contact via 'Get In Touch' CTA
description: Verifies the 'Get In Touch' button routes the user from the hero section to the Contact page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Get In Touch' button in the hero section (element index 102) to route to the Contact page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section/div[2]/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/contact' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Contact').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Contact Form').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Counters animate from 0 to target values when statistics section becomes visible
description: Verifies that each statistic counter starts at 0 and animates upward when the statistics section scrolls into view.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Scroll to top' button to move the counters out of view, then scroll down to the Achievements & Impact section to attempt to retrigger the counters' animation and check for the initial '0' state.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=0').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=0').first).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=1').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Statistic counters do not start at '0' when the statistics section scrolls into view; DOM shows final values (e.g., '100').
- Retrigger attempts (scroll out/in and using 'Scroll to top') performed 3 times did not reset counters to '0' and no '0' start state was observed.
- The animation start-state ('0' then animate upward) could not be verified because the counters never displayed the initial '0' value.

testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Statistics labels 'Years', 'Projects', 'Technologies', and 'Satisfaction' not found on page; the visible statistics section contains different labels ('Major Projects', 'Mobile Responsive', 'Items Managed').
- Multiple attempts to reveal the statistics section by scrolling (3 attempts) did not surface the requested labels or additional statistic tiles.
- No link or interactive element was found that navigates to an alternative statistics area containing the requested labels.

title: TC009-Counters do not animate while the statistics section is not visible (before scrolling)
description: Verifies that counters remain at their initial state until the statistics section is actually scrolled into view.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # --> Assertions to verify final state

        assert '/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//section[@id="statistics"]').first).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//section[@id="hero"]').locator('text=0').first).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//section[@id="statistics"]').locator('text=0').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Statistics counters were not visible in the initial viewport before scrolling, so the initial '0' state could not be observed.
- After scrolling, the statistics counters display "3+", "100%", and "100+"; none of the counters contain the digit "0".

title: TC010-Fast scroll past statistics then scroll back triggers counter animation on visibility
description: Verifies that if the user scrolls past quickly and then returns to the statistics section, the counters animate upon becoming visible.
code:

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        await expect(frame.locator("xpath=//section[contains(translate(., 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ', 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'), 'statistics')]//*[contains(text(),'1') or contains(text(),'2') or contains(text(),'3') or contains(text(),'4') or contains(text(),'5') or contains(text(),'6') or contains(text(),'7') or contains(text(),'8') or contains(text(),'9')]").first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:

            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Counter animation could not be verified from the available DOM and screenshot; only static end-state values are observable.
- No intermediate animation frames or timing indicators were present to confirm animation start or stop when the statistics section was made visible.
- The page displays non-zero counter values (e.g., '3+', '100%', '100+'), but these static values alone do not provide evidence that an on-visibility animation occurred.
- The current testing environment does not expose animation events or logs necessary to conclusively determine that the counters animated and then stopped.

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/about' in frame.url
        assert 'About' in await frame.title()
        await expect(frame.locator('text=About').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Education').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Languages').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC017-Gradient profile image placeholder is visible on the About page
description: Verifies that the profile image area is displayed as a visible placeholder rather than a broken or missing element.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'About' link (element index 66) to navigate to the /about page so assertions can be performed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the About navigation link (use interactive element index 63) to navigate to the /about page so the assertions can be performed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Verify URL contains /about
        assert "/about" in frame.url
        
        # Verify profile image (initials) is visible
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[1]/div/div[1]').is_visible()
        
        # Verify profile card is visible
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[1]/div').is_visible()
        
        # Gradient placeholder element is not present in the provided available elements list
        assert False, "Gradient placeholder element not found in available elements; feature missing."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode

        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

title: TC022-Live badge is visible on projects marked as active
description: Verifies that projects intended to be active display a visible "Live" badge in the project card.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'About' link to navigate to the /about page (use the in-page About link) so the project showcase on that page can be examined.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the in-page 'About' link (index 64) to navigate to /about so the project showcase can be examined.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the in-page About link (index 67) to open /about, scroll to the Featured Projects/project cards, verify the 'Live' badge is visible on project cards and that it appears inside the project card area, then finish the task.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/nav/div/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Verify we are on the /about page
        assert "/about" in frame.url
        
        # Check visibility of 'Live' badges on each project card (use exact xpaths)
        live1 = await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div[1]/div[1]/span').is_visible()
        live2 = await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div[2]/div[1]/span').is_visible()
        live3 = await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/div[1]/span').is_visible()
        
        assert any([live1, live2, live3]), 'Expected at least one "Live" badge to be visible on a project card.'
        
        # For any visible badge, assert the corresponding project card container is visible (ensures badge is inside project area, not header/footer)
        if live1:
            assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div[1]').is_visible(), 'The "Live" badge for the first project is not inside the project card area.'
        if live2:
            assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div[2]').is_visible(), 'The "Live" badge for the second project is not inside the project card area.'
        if live3:
            assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/div/div[3]').is_visible(), 'The "Live" badge for the third project is not inside the project card area.'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
